response:
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"

SECTION 1  
An innocent person, upon receiving a false verdict, might feel a mix of confusion and disbelief. The weight of the moment could be overwhelming, and the words that come out might be uncertain. "I didn’t do this," could be the first thing they say, echoing through the courtroom. The lead of the situation is heavy, and the silver lining seems to be absent. It’s a moment that changes everything, and the person might feel lost in the chaos.

SECTION 2  
Sir, I beg your pardon, but I didn’t commit the crime. The stranger in the room might have been the last one to see me, and I can’t shake the feeling that someone is trying to frame me. I need to be heard, and I need the truth to come out. The wrap of this situation is not fair, and I’m not giving up. I will fight for my innocence, no matter the cost.
